LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. APPEARANCE FOR DEFENDANT-MOVING PARTY & FOR PLAINTIFF-OPPOSING PARTY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULE DEMURRER 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION - TWO WARNINGS NOT PROTECTED BY "INTERESTED PERSON" EXCEPTION PER CCP SEC. 47(C) IF MOTIVATED BY HATRED OR ILL WILL. GRANT MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVES WITHOUT PREJUDICE PER CCP 425.14. GRANT MOTION TO STRIKE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ADDITIONAL NAMES OF SALVATION ARMY. 10 DAYS TO FILE ANSWER. HON. RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. REPORTER: RHONDA L. AQUILINA, CSR 	956

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE SALVATION ARMY, JOHN BOATMAN and CARLO DE LISO (INCORRECTLY SUED AS CARLOS LISO) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES:
APPEAR: Kenneth N. Frucht, Esq. 392-4844 for pltf & opposing party; Carla J. Feldman, Esq. 310/282-2000 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (DEMURRER:
FEHA CAUSES OF ACTION - 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION UNDER THE FEDERAL AMERICAN WITH DISABILITIES ACT AND LABOR CODE SECTION 132A.
DEFAMATION - 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE MALICE.
INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS - 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE.
MOTION TO STRIKE GRANT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEK LEAVE OF COURT FOR PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT). Deft to submit order. (302/AJR/AA) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193
